preamble: The shift from 'pʲ' to 'f' represents a complex case of lenition combined with a change in articulation. Palatalized bilabial stops can weaken into labiodental fricatives through intermediate stages involving labialization or palatal frication.

examples.note: Historical developments where palatalized bilabials weaken to labiodental fricatives in specific contexts or dialectal variations.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
